channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Hezbollah steps up rocket fire as Israel sends more troops into Lebanon
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Hezbollah fired another barrage of rockets into Israel on Tuesday, and the militant group’s acting leader vowed to keep up pressure that has forced tens of thousands of Israelis from their homes near the Lebanese border.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE and TIA GOLDENBERG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Pioneers in artificial intelligence win the Nobel Prize in physics
item.description: STOCKHOLM (AP) — Two pioneers of artificial intelligence — John Hopfield and Geoffrey Hinton — won the Nobel Prize in physics Tuesday for helping create the building blocks of machine learning that is revolutionizing the way we work and live but also creates new threats for humanity.
item.dc:creator: DANIEL NIEMANN, SETH BORENSTEIN and MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Survivors of massacre in small Haitian town where 70 died point finger at government
item.description: PONT-SONDÉ, Haiti (AP) — Angry whispers have broken the heavy silence that fell over Pont-Sondé just days after a vicious gang attack left more than 70 dead, marking one of Haiti’s biggest massacres in recent history.
item.dc:creator: PIERRE-RICHARD LUXAMA and DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mexico's president lays out a plan to combat cartel violence. But it looks like more of the same
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Mexico’s new president laid out a plan Tuesday to combat drug cartel violence, but analysts say it appears to be largely a continuation of previous policy.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden called Netanyahu 'a bad f---ing guy': Woodward book
item.description: President Biden’s frustrations with Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu over the war in Gaza have at times boiled over in profane terms, according to accounts in a new book by Pulitzer Prize-winning journalist Bob Woodward.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Hungary’s Orban interrupted, accused of 'selling out' country to Russia, China during EU news conference
item.description: Hungary's Prime Minister Viktor Orban was interrupted at a news conference by an activist and municipal councilor from the opposition Democratic Coalition party.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Travelers from Rwanda to be screened for Marburg
item.description: U.S. health officials are planning to screen travelers from Rwanda amid the outbreak of the Marburg virus in the country, the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) announced on Monday. The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) is set to begin scanning the week of Oct.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Hungary’s Orbán interrupted by activist during EU presidency news conference
item.description: An activist and municipal councilor for a Hungarian opposition party disrupted a news conference in the French city of Strasbourg, as Prime Minister Viktor Orbán was laying out plans for Hungary’s presidency of the European Union.
item.dc:creator: Justin Spike, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump campaign blasts Woodward; denies secret Putin calls
item.description: Former President Trump's campaign is rejecting accounts in journalist Bob Woodward's new book that the Republican presidential nominee has held multiple phone calls with Russian President Vladimir Putin since leaving office in January 2021.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: German leader Scholz's party moves fast to replace an official in a key campaign post
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — German Chancellor Olaf Scholz's party moved quickly to replace a top official who stepped down less than a year ahead of a challenging national election, presenting a well-connected left-wing lawmaker on Tuesday as his replacement in a post that is important for the campaign.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: MI5 spy chief says Russia and Iran are behind a 'staggering' rise in deadly plots
item.description: The head of MI5 says Britain is facing a staggering rise in attempts at assassination, sabotage and other crimes backed by Russia and Iran.
item.dc:creator: Jill Lawless, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Former Taiwan leader Tsai to visit Czech Republic, other European destinations, drawing China's ire
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — Former Taiwan leader Tsai Ing-wen is to visit Czech Republic, and other European destinations, drawing China’s ire.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: German court acquits McCann suspect of unrelated sexual offense charges
item.description: BRAUNSCHWEIG, Germany (AP) — A German court on Tuesday acquitted a man who is also under investigation in the 2007 disappearance of British toddler Madeleine McCann in a trial on charges of unrelated sexual offenses.
item.dc:creator: FANNY BRODERSEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Water gushes through palm trees and sand dunes after rare rain in the Sahara Desert
item.description: RABAT, Morocco (AP) — A rare deluge of rainfall left blue lagoons of water amid the palm trees and sand dunes of the Sahara desert, nourishing some of its most drought-stricken regions with more water than many had seen in decades.
item.dc:creator: SAM METZ and BABA AHMED, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: France’s minority government survives a no-confidence vote, 2 weeks after taking office
item.description: France’s minority government has survived a no-confidence vote, two weeks after taking office, getting over the first hurdle placed by left-wing lawmakers to bring down new conservative Prime Minister Michel Barnier.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Russian court sentences a 72-year-old American to nearly 7 years in prison for fighting in Ukraine
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — A Russian court on Monday sentenced a 72-year-old American in a closed trial to nearly seven years in prison for allegedly fighting as a mercenary in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Vigils, protests around the world commemorate the anniversary of Hamas attack on Israel
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Commemorations and protests unfolded across the world on Monday to mark the anniversary of the Oct.
item.dc:creator: DAVID R. MARTIN, TASSANEE VEJPONGSA and JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The mayor of a state capital in Mexico is killed less than 1 week after he took office
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Relatives on Monday buried the mayor of a state capital in southern Mexico who was killed just one week after he took office. Photos of the crime scene shared on social media showed Mayor Alejandro Arcos’ severed head had been left on the roof of a pickup truck.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tunisia's Kais Saied wins landslide reelection, entrenching his power in Arab Spring's birthplace
item.description: TUNIS, Tunisia (AP) — President Kais Saied won a landslide victory in Tunisia's election Monday, keeping his grip on power after a first term in which opponents were imprisoned and the country's institutions overhauled to give him more authority.
item.dc:creator: BOUAZZA BEN BOUAZZA and SAM METZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli military warns of operations on southern Lebanon's coast
item.description: Israel's military on Monday said it would soon launch operations on Lebanon’s southern coast, which would mark a further escalation of its fight against Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: Watch: Harris, Emhoff mark anniversary of Oct. 7 attacks
item.description: Vice President Harris and second gentleman Doug Emhoff on Monday marked the first anniversary of the Oct. 7, 2023, attacks by Hamas on Israel. The two were expected to plant a memorial tree at the vice president’s residence, in addition to delivering remarks to the press.
item.dc:creator: The Hill

item.title: Benjamin Netanyahu's Churchill moment comes amid multifront war against Iranian terror, expert says
item.description: Netanyahu is facing his greatest challenge as Israel’s longest-serving Prime Minister while leading the Jewish state in an existential war against the county’s Islamist terrorist enemies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The world's rivers faced the driest year in three decades in 2023, the UN weather agency says
item.description: GENEVA (AP) — The U.N. weather agency is reporting that 2023 was the driest year in more than three decades for the world's rivers, as the record-hot year underpinned a drying up of water flows and contributed to prolonged droughts in some places.
item.dc:creator: JAMEY KEATEN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Saving lives on 'Death Street,' how an Israeli kindergarten teacher became a battlefield hero on October 7
item.description: When Hamas terrorists attacked her hometown in Southern Israel, Tali Hadad a kindergarten teacher, leapt into action to save 13 people, including her own wounded son.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Swiss probe into woman's voluntary death causing advocacy groups behind 'suicide capsule' to suspend its use
item.description: Advocacy groups behind a "suicide capsule" said they have suspended applications to use it until Swiss authorities complete a criminal probe into the death from its first use.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Abigail Edan's aunt says 'we need a deal' on Oct. 7 anniversary
item.description: "I say this to the Palestinian people, and I say this to the Israelis Diplomacy now is what we need," Naftali said.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: New report finds nearly 200% increase in antisemitic incidents in US since Oct. 7 Hamas terror massacre
item.description: The Anti-Defamation League recorded around 10,000 antisemitic incidents since Oct. 7, marking the largest year-to-year increase since the group started recording such incidents in 1979.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Expert warns UN's role in AI regulation could lead to safety overreach
item.description: Nations have pursued a number of options to better globally coordinate artificial intelligence safety initiatives, including several safety summits and the creation of safety institutes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New MH370 project searches for answers on vanished plane
item.description: A new project is searching for answers about what happened to Malaysian Airlines Flight MH370 and its 239 passengers.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: Middle East latest: An Israeli airstrike cuts a major highway linking Lebanon with Syria
item.description: An Israeli airstrike has cut off a main highway linking Lebanon with Syria, leaving two huge craters on either side of the road.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: New NATO chief Mark Rutte visits Ukraine in his first trip since taking office
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — New NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte visited Ukraine on Thursday in his first official trip since taking office and pledging the alliance's continued support for Kyiv in its war with Russia.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA and SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli strike in Beirut killed 7 health and rescue workers
item.description: An Israeli strike overnight in the Lebanese capital Beirut killed seven health and rescue workers, an Islamic health organization said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation
